directives.subject: Wing Spar Attachment Fitting — Inspection
directives.summary: Mandatory inspection of wing spar attachment fittings on A320 family aircraft with more than 15,000 flight cycles.
directives.applicability: Airbus A318, A319, A320, A321 — all serial numbers
directives.compliance: Within 6 months of effective date

directives.subject: Fuel Quantity Indication System — Software Update
directives.summary: Installation of updated fuel quantity indication software to address erroneous low-level fuel warnings during cruise flight.
directives.applicability: Airbus A350-900 and A350-1000, all production serial numbers up to MSN 650
directives.compliance: Within 12 months of effective date

directives.subject: Nose Landing Gear Steering Actuator — Replacement
directives.summary: Replacement of nose landing gear steering actuators manufactured in a specific production batch due to potential internal seal degradation.
directives.applicability: Airbus A330-200, A330-300, A330-800, A330-900 with affected actuator part numbers
directives.compliance: Within 3 months or 1,500 flight hours, whichever occurs first

directives.subject: Fuselage Mid-Body Door Plug — Inspection and Fastener Torque Check
directives.summary: Repetitive detailed inspections and fastener torque checks of the mid-body fuselage door plug area to detect potential loosening of attachment bolts.
directives.applicability: Boeing 737-8 and 737-9 aircraft, line numbers 1 through 1500
directives.compliance: Initial inspection within 90 days; repetitive every 1,000 flight cycles

directives.subject: Engine Pylon Forward Fairing — Structural Reinforcement
directives.summary: Structural reinforcement of the engine pylon forward fairing to prevent fatigue cracking at the attachment rib station.
directives.applicability: Boeing 777-200, 777-200LR, 777-300, 777-300ER with GE90 engines
directives.compliance: Within 18 months of effective date

directives.subject: Horizontal Stabiliser Attachment Bolts — Emergency Inspection
directives.summary: Emergency airworthiness directive requiring inspection of horizontal stabiliser attachment bolts following reports of bolt migration in service.
directives.applicability: Boeing 787-8 and 787-9, all serial numbers
directives.compliance: Before next flight

directives.subject: Propeller Blade Retention — Torque Check
directives.summary: Repetitive torque check of propeller blade retention nuts to prevent potential blade separation due to insufficient clamping force.
directives.applicability: ATR 72-200, ATR 72-500, ATR 72-600 — all serial numbers
directives.compliance: Within 500 flight hours or 6 months, whichever occurs first

directives.subject: Main Landing Gear Side Brace — Ultrasonic Inspection
directives.summary: Mandatory ultrasonic inspection of the main landing gear side brace link to detect fatigue cracking at the pivot bore.
directives.applicability: ATR 42-300, ATR 42-500, ATR 42-600 — all serial numbers above MSN 100
directives.compliance: Within 4 months of effective date

directives.subject: Flight Control Computer — Software Update
directives.summary: Mandatory software update for the primary flight control computer to correct a logic fault in the yaw damper augmentation system.
directives.applicability: Embraer E190-E2 and E195-E2, all serial numbers
directives.compliance: Within 90 days of effective date

directives.subject: Wing Lower Skin Panel — Eddy Current Inspection
directives.summary: Eddy current inspection of wing lower skin panels in the area of the main landing gear attachment to detect stress corrosion cracking.
directives.applicability: Embraer ERJ 135, ERJ 140, ERJ 145 — all serial numbers
directives.compliance: Within 12 months or at next C-check, whichever occurs first

directives.subject: Wing Trailing Edge Flap Actuator — Replacement
directives.summary: Replacement of wing trailing edge flap actuators from a specific production lot due to potential bearing seizure under high-cycle fatigue.
directives.applicability: Gulfstream G650 and G650ER, serial numbers 6001 through 6350
directives.compliance: Within 6 months or 2,000 flight hours, whichever occurs first

directives.subject: Auxiliary Power Unit Fire Detection Loop — Wiring Check
directives.summary: Inspection and rerouting of APU fire detection loop wiring to prevent potential chafing against hydraulic lines in the aft fuselage compartment.
directives.applicability: Gulfstream G700, all serial numbers up to 77050
directives.compliance: Within 30 days of effective date

directives.subject: Fuselage Crown Skin — Lap Joint Inspection
directives.summary: Detailed visual and non-destructive inspection of fuselage crown skin lap joints at frames 22 through 38 for fatigue cracking.
directives.applicability: Bombardier CRJ-700, CRJ-900, CRJ-1000 — all serial numbers
directives.compliance: Within 12 months or 4,000 flight cycles, whichever occurs first

directives.subject: Hydraulic System Pressure Relief Valve — Replacement
directives.summary: Replacement of hydraulic system pressure relief valves manufactured by a specific supplier due to premature spring failure causing system depressurisation.
directives.applicability: Bombardier Global 7500, serial numbers 70001 through 70085
directives.compliance: Within 500 flight hours or 3 months, whichever occurs first

directives.subject: Rudder Servo Actuator — Functional Test
directives.summary: Repetitive functional test of the rudder servo actuator to verify correct authority limits following a design review of the hydraulic servo valve.
directives.applicability: Dassault Falcon 900, Falcon 900EX, Falcon 900LX — all serial numbers
directives.compliance: Within 200 flight hours or at next scheduled maintenance, whichever occurs first

directives.subject: Engine Fuel Feed Manifold — Emergency Inspection
directives.summary: Emergency airworthiness directive for inspection of engine fuel feed manifold brazing joints following two in-service fuel leak events.
directives.applicability: Dassault Falcon 8X, all serial numbers equipped with PW307D engines
directives.compliance: Before next flight

directives.subject: Elevator Trim Tab Hinge — Inspection and Lubrication
directives.summary: Inspection and lubrication of elevator trim tab hinge assemblies to prevent binding caused by corrosion in high-humidity environments.
directives.applicability: Cessna Citation CJ3+, CJ4, all serial numbers
directives.compliance: Within 300 flight hours or 6 months, whichever occurs first

directives.subject: Wing De-Ice Boot Bonding — Rework
directives.summary: Rework of wing leading edge de-ice boot bonding following reports of boot delamination during flight in icing conditions.
directives.applicability: Beechcraft King Air 250, King Air 350, King Air 350ER — all serial numbers
directives.compliance: Before the next flight into known icing conditions or within 60 days, whichever occurs first

directives.subject: Main Landing Gear Trunnion — Magnetic Particle Inspection
directives.summary: Magnetic particle inspection of the main landing gear trunnion fitting to detect fatigue cracking at the upper attachment lug.
directives.applicability: Pilatus PC-12, PC-12/45, PC-12/47, PC-12/47E — all serial numbers
directives.compliance: Within 1,000 flight hours or 12 months, whichever occurs first

directives.subject: Avionics Cooling Fan — Replacement
directives.summary: Replacement of the avionics bay cooling fan assembly due to potential motor bearing seizure leading to overheating of flight management computers.
directives.applicability: Pilatus PC-24, serial numbers 101 through 350
directives.compliance: Within 500 flight hours or 6 months, whichever occurs first

directives.subject: Over-the-Wing Engine Mount — Bolt Torque Verification
directives.summary: Torque verification of the over-the-wing engine mount attachment bolts to address a manufacturing tolerance issue in the bolt shanks.
directives.applicability: HondaJet HA-420, all variants including Elite and Elite S, all serial numbers
directives.compliance: Within 200 flight hours or 90 days, whichever occurs first

directives.subject: Nose Landing Gear Retraction Actuator — Inspection
directives.summary: Inspection of nose landing gear retraction actuator for potential hydraulic fluid leak at the cylinder head seal.
directives.applicability: Honda Aircraft HondaJet HA-420, Elite and Elite S variants
directives.compliance: Within 500 flight hours or 6 months, whichever occurs first

directives.subject: Cargo Door Locking Mechanism — Rigging Check
directives.summary: Rigging check and adjustment of the forward cargo door locking mechanism to ensure correct over-centre latching under structural deflection.
directives.applicability: Boeing 747-8F, serial numbers RC501 through RC580
directives.compliance: Within 12 months of effective date

directives.subject: Outboard Leading Edge Slat Tracks — Detailed Inspection
directives.summary: Detailed inspection of outboard leading edge slat tracks at rib positions 14 and 16 for stress corrosion cracking in high-humidity operational environments.
directives.applicability: Boeing 737-600, 737-700, 737-800, 737-900 — all serial numbers
directives.compliance: Within 6,000 flight cycles or 18 months, whichever occurs first

directives.subject: Flap Asymmetry Protection System — Functional Test
directives.summary: Repetitive functional test of the flap asymmetry protection system following reports of uncommanded flap retraction during approach.
directives.applicability: ATR 72-500 and ATR 72-600, all serial numbers
directives.compliance: Within 30 days and repetitive every 2,000 flight hours

directives.subject: Wing Rib Foot — Fatigue Crack Inspection
directives.summary: Ultrasonic inspection of wing rib feet at stations 5 through 12 for fatigue cracking detected during fleet leader programme.
directives.applicability: Airbus A380-800, all serial numbers with more than 8,000 flight cycles
directives.compliance: Within 24 months or at next D-check, whichever occurs first

directives.subject: Fuel Selector Valve — Operational Check
directives.summary: Operational check and replacement if necessary of the fuel selector valve to address reports of incomplete sealing in the off position.
directives.applicability: Cessna 208 and 208B Caravan, all serial numbers
directives.compliance: Within 100 flight hours or 30 days, whichever occurs first

directives.subject: Horizontal Stabiliser Trim Actuator — Inspection
directives.summary: Inspection of horizontal stabiliser trim actuator gearbox for wear on the worm gear teeth detected during routine overhaul.
directives.applicability: Bombardier Challenger 300 and Challenger 350, all serial numbers
directives.compliance: Within 2,000 flight hours or 18 months, whichever occurs first

directives.subject: Emergency Airworthiness Directive — Engine Bleed Air Duct
directives.summary: Emergency AD requiring inspection of engine bleed air ducts for thermal damage following two in-service duct rupture events.
directives.applicability: Embraer E170, E175, E190, E195 — all variants
directives.compliance: Before next flight

directives.subject: Bleed Air Pre-Cooler — Leak Test
directives.summary: Mandatory leak test of the bleed air pre-cooler ducting following reports of cabin pressurisation anomalies traced to duct joint failures.
directives.applicability: Airbus A319, A320, A321 — serial numbers with CFM56 engines
directives.compliance: Within 3 months of effective date

directives.subject: Oxygen System Distribution Manifold — Inspection
directives.summary: Inspection of the crew and passenger oxygen distribution manifold for corrosion and potential blockage at the flow regulator junction.
directives.applicability: Gulfstream G500, G550 — all serial numbers
directives.compliance: Within 12 months of effective date

directives.subject: Aileron Hinge Bracket — Replacement
directives.summary: Replacement of aileron hinge brackets on affected aircraft due to incorrect heat treatment during manufacture resulting in reduced fatigue life.
directives.applicability: Dassault Falcon 2000, Falcon 2000EX, Falcon 2000LXS — serial numbers 1 through 350
directives.compliance: Within 1,000 flight hours or 12 months, whichever occurs first

directives.subject: Empennage Attachment Bolts — Inspection
directives.summary: Inspection of empennage to fuselage attachment bolts for elongation and corrosion, particularly on aircraft operating in tropical environments.
directives.applicability: Cessna 208 and 208B Caravan, all serial numbers
directives.compliance: Within 500 flight hours or 6 months, whichever occurs first

directives.subject: Engine Vibration Monitoring System — Calibration Update
directives.summary: Calibration update of the engine vibration monitoring system to correct threshold settings that may mask abnormal vibration indications on PW1500G engines.
directives.applicability: Airbus A220-100 and A220-300, all serial numbers
directives.compliance: Within 6 months of effective date

directives.subject: Wing Centre Section Lower Skin — Fatigue Inspection
directives.summary: Repetitive detailed inspection of the wing centre section lower skin for fatigue cracking at the rear spar chord attachment.
directives.applicability: Boeing 767-200, 767-300, 767-300F, 767-400ER — high-cycle aircraft with more than 20,000 flight cycles
directives.compliance: Within 12 months and repetitive every 6,000 flight cycles

directives.subject: Cabin Pressure Controller — Software Correction
directives.summary: Software correction for the cabin pressure controller to address an intermittent fault that may cause rapid cabin altitude change indications on the ECAM.
directives.applicability: Airbus A321neo, all variants equipped with CPCS software version 4.2 or earlier
directives.compliance: Within 3 months of effective date

directives.subject: Main Landing Gear Truck Beam — Inspection
directives.summary: Non-destructive inspection of main landing gear truck beam for stress corrosion cracking at the axle attachment journal.
directives.applicability: Boeing 757-200 and 757-300, all serial numbers with more than 30,000 flight cycles
directives.compliance: Within 12 months or at next landing gear overhaul, whichever occurs first

directives.subject: Vertical Stabiliser Attachment — Torque Check
directives.summary: Torque check of vertical stabiliser to fuselage attachment bolts following fleet survey findings of under-torqued fasteners.
directives.applicability: Embraer E190 and E195, all serial numbers
directives.compliance: Within 60 days of effective date

directives.subject: Propeller Governor — Overspeed Test
directives.summary: Repetitive propeller governor overspeed test following identification of a batch of governors with incorrect spring tension settings.
directives.applicability: Beechcraft King Air 250, King Air 350 — serial numbers with affected governors
directives.compliance: Within 100 flight hours or 30 days, whichever occurs first; repetitive every 500 hours

directives.subject: Fuel Tank Vent Valve — Replacement
directives.summary: Replacement of fuel tank vent valves with an improved design following incidents of valve sticking in the closed position during descent.
directives.applicability: Pilatus PC-12 NG and PC-12 NGX, serial numbers 1001 through 2100
directives.compliance: Within 300 flight hours or 6 months, whichever occurs first

directives.subject: Windshield Anti-Ice Controller — Circuit Board Replacement
directives.summary: Replacement of windshield anti-ice controller circuit boards due to a capacitor reliability issue that may result in loss of windshield heating.
directives.applicability: Bombardier Global 6000 and Global 6500, serial numbers with affected controller part number
directives.compliance: Within 6 months of effective date

directives.subject: Spoiler Actuator Rod End Bearing — Replacement
directives.summary: Replacement of spoiler actuator rod end bearings with an improved design due to premature wear in the spherical bearing surface.
directives.applicability: Dassault Falcon 7X, all serial numbers
directives.compliance: Within 1,500 flight hours or 18 months, whichever occurs first

directives.subject: Fuel Quantity Probe — Calibration Verification
directives.summary: Verification of fuel quantity probe calibration following reports of inaccurate fuel indications at low fuel states on Australian-registered aircraft.
directives.applicability: Honda Aircraft HondaJet HA-420, all variants
directives.compliance: Within 90 days of effective date

directives.subject: Tail Cone Fire Extinguisher Bottle — Hydrostatic Test
directives.summary: Hydrostatic test or replacement of tail cone APU fire extinguisher bottles manufactured before January 2020 due to pressure vessel integrity concerns.
directives.applicability: Gulfstream G650 and G650ER, all serial numbers with affected bottle part numbers
directives.compliance: Within 12 months of effective date

directives.subject: Rudder Control System — Emergency Inspection of Rollover Mechanism
directives.summary: Emergency airworthiness directive for inspection of the rudder control system rollover mechanism following a reported jammed rudder event during approach.
directives.applicability: Boeing 737-7, 737-8, 737-9 — all serial numbers
directives.compliance: Before next flight

directives.subject: Slat Track Roller — Inspection for Wear
directives.summary: Inspection of slat track rollers for excessive wear that may lead to slat binding or asymmetric deployment during approach configuration.
directives.applicability: Airbus A330-800 and A330-900, all serial numbers
directives.compliance: Within 6 months or 3,000 flight cycles, whichever occurs first

directives.subject: Elevator Control Cable — Tension Adjustment
directives.summary: Adjustment of elevator control cable tension to revised limits following discovery of incorrect tension values in the maintenance manual.
directives.applicability: ATR 72-200 and ATR 72-500, all serial numbers
directives.compliance: Within 200 flight hours or 3 months, whichever occurs first

directives.subject: Emergency AD — Seat Rail Locking Pin
directives.summary: Emergency airworthiness directive requiring inspection and replacement of pilot and co-pilot seat rail locking pins following reports of seat slippage during takeoff.
directives.applicability: Cessna 172R, 172S, and 172SP, manufactured between 2018 and 2024
directives.compliance: Before next flight

directives.subject: Referenced in 2026-0041: Wing Spar Attachment Fitting — Inspection
directives.summary: Service Bulletin referenced by EASA AD 2026-0041
directives.applicability: Airbus A318, A319, A320, A321 — all serial numbers

directives.subject: Referenced in AD 2026-04-11: Fuselage Mid-Body Door Plug — Inspection and Fastener Torque Check
directives.summary: Service Bulletin referenced by FAA AD 2026-04-11
directives.applicability: Boeing 737-8 and 737-9 aircraft

directives.subject: Referenced in 2025-0198: Fuel Quantity Indication System — Software Update
directives.summary: Service Bulletin referenced by EASA AD 2025-0198
directives.applicability: Airbus A350-900 and A350-1000

directives.subject: Referenced in 2026-0008: Rudder Control System — Emergency Inspection of Rollover Mechanism
directives.summary: Service Bulletin referenced by EASA AD 2026-0008

directives.subject: Referenced in AD 2026-03-08: Engine Bleed Air Duct — Inspection
directives.summary: Service Bulletin referenced by FAA AD 2026-03-08
directives.applicability: Embraer E170, E175, E190, E195 — all variants

directives.subject: Fuselage Skin Panel — Corrosion Prevention Compound Application
directives.summary: Application of corrosion prevention compound to fuselage skin panels in the belly fairing area to extend structural inspection intervals.
directives.applicability: Airbus A319, A320, A321 — all serial numbers with more than 20,000 flight cycles
directives.compliance: Recommended within 24 months

directives.subject: Engine Fan Blade — Borescope Inspection Enhancement
directives.summary: Enhanced borescope inspection procedure for GE90 engine fan blades to improve detection of leading edge erosion and foreign object damage.
directives.applicability: Boeing 777-200, 777-300, 777-200LR, 777-300ER with GE90 engines
directives.compliance: Recommended at next scheduled engine borescope inspection
